info.name: Bitmap
info.comment: Represents a pixel buffer used for drawing, image transfer and display backing.

description.p: The Bitmap class describes a rectangular block of pixel data together with its dimensions, colour format, palette, clipping region and drawing state. Bitmaps are used directly by Display and Picture objects and provide the low-level pixel storage behind much of Kōtuku's 2D graphics pipeline.

To create a bitmap, set Width and Height before initialisation. The pixel format can be selected explicitly with BitsPerPixel, BytesPerPixel, AmtColours and Type, or left for Query and Init to derive from the current display environment. DataFlags controls whether the bitmap uses regular CPU-accessible memory or a platform-specific video or texture resource where supported.

Direct CPU access is reliable for regular data bitmaps. Bitmaps backed by video or texture resources may require Lock before reading or writing Data, and Unlock after direct access is complete. Code that uses the drawing methods exposed by this class does not normally need to manage locking itself.

Bitmap methods are intentionally low-level and operate on immediate pixel data. Use the Vector module when retained scene graphs, paths, gradients, filters or higher-level drawing composition are required. Use Picture when decoding or encoding image formats is the main concern.
Raw image bytes can be read and written with Read and Write. SaveImage writes the clipped bitmap image as PCX data to a destination object that supports writing.

action.name: Clear
action.comment: Clears the bitmap image to BkgdIndex.

description.p: Clear fills the full bitmap with the current background colour. The colour used by the operation is BkgdIndex, which is derived from Bkgd when the background colour is set through the RGB field.

To clear a bitmap to a different colour without changing the background fields, call DrawRectangle with BAF::FILL. For alpha-capable bitmaps, setting BkgdIndex to zero is an efficient way to clear the image to transparent black.

result.error: Failed to lock a required resource

action.name: CopyData
action.comment: Copies bitmap image data to other bitmaps with colour remapping enabled.

input.param: The unique ID of the destination object.

description.p: CopyData copies this bitmap into another initialised Bitmap object. Other destination classes are not supported.
The copy is clipped to the destination dimensions. If the destination is wider or taller than the source, the exposed area is cleared to the destination bitmap's background colour.

action.name: Draw
action.comment: Clears the bitmap image to BkgdIndex.
action.prototype: ERR acDraw(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height)

input.param: The X position of the region to be drawn.
The Y position of the region to be drawn.
The width of the region to be drawn.
The height of the region to be drawn.

description.p: Draw fills the full bitmap with the current background colour. It is equivalent to drawing a filled rectangle over the entire bitmap with BkgdIndex.

action.name: Flush
action.comment: Flushes pending graphics operations and returns when the accelerator is idle.

description.p: Flush synchronises pending graphics operations with the active graphics backend. Synchronisation is required before direct CPU access to accelerator-managed bitmap memory.
Clients do not need to call this function if solely using the graphics methods provided in the Bitmap class.

Init prepares a queried bitmap for use. It validates the calculated bitmap state, allocates Data when required, configures platform-specific backing resources and selects the pixel access routines used by drawing operations.
If Data has already been supplied, Init uses the caller-provided memory. Otherwise allocation is controlled by DataFlags and Flags. Width and Height must be set before this action is called.

result.error: A required field value is undefined
AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block
A call to the host system has failed

action.name: Lock
action.comment: Locks the bitmap surface for direct read/write access.

description.p: Lock makes bitmap memory available through Data for direct CPU access. It is mainly required for bitmaps backed by a video or platform drawable resource; data-backed bitmaps are already CPU-accessible.
Call Unlock when direct access is complete so platform resources can be released or synchronised.

result.error: No data is available for use
Failed to lock a required resource
A required field value is undefined
AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block
A call to the host system has failed
Failed to create a new resource

action.name: Query
action.comment: Populates a bitmap with pre-initialised/default values prior to initialisation.

description.p: Query calculates the bitmap's derived fields without allocating image memory. It resolves values such as Type, BytesPerPixel, BitsPerPixel, AmtColours, ByteWidth, LineWidth, PlaneMod and Size from the fields already set by the caller.
At minimum, Width and Height must be positive. If format fields are incomplete, Query derives a compatible format where possible; for example, BytesPerPixel set to 2 implies a 16-bit bitmap.

action.name: Read
action.comment: Reads raw image data from a bitmap object.
action.prototype: ERR acRead(*Object, APTR Buffer, INT Length, INT *Result)

input.param: Points a buffer that will receive the data.
The total number of bytes to read from the object. This value cannot exceed the size of the Buffer.
The Read action will write this parameter with the total number of bytes read into the Buffer.

description.p: Read copies bytes from Data into the supplied output buffer, starting at Position. Position is advanced by the number of bytes copied and the result count is returned in the action arguments.
If the requested length would pass the end of the bitmap data, Read truncates the transfer to the remaining byte count.

result.error: No data is available for use
A value is outside of the valid range

action.name: Resize
action.prototype: ERR acResize(*Object, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height, DOUBLE Depth)

input.param: The new width of the object.
The new height of the object.
The new depth of the object.

description.p: Resize changes Width, Height and, unless BMF::FIXED_DEPTH is set, BitsPerPixel. Existing image content is not preserved.
If BMF::NEVER_SHRINK is set, requested dimensions smaller than the current bitmap are raised to the current size. If BMF::CLEAR is set, the resized bitmap is cleared to Bkgd.

result.error: A required field value is undefined
AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block

action.name: SaveImage
action.comment: Saves the bitmap image to a writable object in PCX format.
action.prototype: ERR acSaveImage(*Object, OBJECTID Dest, CLASSID ClassID)

input.param: Refers to an object that will receive the encoded image data.
The Picture class to use for encoding the image data.

description.p: SaveImage writes the current clipping region to Dest as PCX image data. Paletted bitmaps are written with a palette; true-colour bitmaps are written as three colour planes. If ColourSpace is CS::LINEAR_RGB, RGB values are converted to sRGB while the image is written.
Errors returned by the destination object's Write action are propagated to the caller.

result.error: AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block

action.name: Seek
action.comment: Changes the current byte position for read/write operations.
action.prototype: ERR acSeek(*Object, DOUBLE Offset, INT Position)

input.param: The desired offset to seek to, relative to the Position parameter.
The position that defines the starting point for Offset.

description.p: Seek sets Position from the supplied byte offset and origin. Positions before the start of the bitmap are clamped to zero, and positions beyond Size are clamped to Size.

action.name: Unlock
action.comment: Unlocks the bitmap surface once direct access is no longer required.

description.p: Unlock releases or synchronises any platform resources held for direct CPU access after Lock.

action.name: Write
action.comment: Writes raw image data to a bitmap object.
action.prototype: ERR acWrite(*Object, APTR Buffer, INT Length, INT Result)

input.param: A buffer containing the data that will be written to the object.
The total number of bytes to write to the object.
This parameter with be updated with the total number of bytes written from the Buffer.

description.p: Write copies bytes from the supplied input buffer into Data, starting at Position. Position is advanced by the number of bytes written and the result count is returned in the action arguments.
The write must fit within the bitmap's allocated Size. Use Seek to change the target position before writing.

result.error: No data is available for use
Out of space. There is no available room to complete the request

method.name: Compress
method.comment: Compresses bitmap data to save memory.

input.param: Reserved compression level. The current implementation uses the compression module's default level.

description.p: Compress() stores a compressed copy of the bitmap's pixel data and releases the uncompressed Data buffer. It is useful for large data-backed bitmaps that need to remain available but are accessed infrequently.
Once a bitmap is compressed, Data is no longer available for direct access. Call Decompress before reading pixels, writing pixels or passing the bitmap to code that expects an active data buffer.
The BMF::COMPRESSED flag is set in Flags after successful compression. Video and texture-backed bitmaps are not supported by this method.

result.error: AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block
The reallocation of a memory block failed
A Compression object could not be created.
Unspecified failure during compression of a data stream

method.name: ConvertToLinear
method.comment: Converts a bitmap's colour space to linear RGB.

description.p: ConvertToLinear() converts the bitmap's clipped region from sRGB to linear RGB. If BMF::ALPHA_CHANNEL is set, pixels with an alpha value of zero are left unchanged.
ColourSpace is set to CS::LINEAR_RGB on completion. The method returns ERR::NothingDone if the bitmap is already marked as linear RGB.
This method currently requires a 32-bit bitmap.

result.error: The Bitmap's content is already in linear RGB format.
The Bitmap is not in the expected state.

method.name: ConvertToRGB
method.comment: Converts a bitmap's colour space to standard RGB.

description.p: ConvertToRGB() converts the bitmap's clipped region from linear RGB to sRGB. If BMF::ALPHA_CHANNEL is set, pixels with an alpha value of zero are left unchanged.
ColourSpace is set to CS::SRGB on completion. The method returns ERR::NothingDone if the bitmap is already marked as sRGB.
This method currently requires a 32-bit bitmap.

result.error: The bitmap's content is already in sRGB format.
The bitmap is not in the expected state.

method.name: CopyArea
method.comment: Copies a rectangular area from one bitmap to another.
method.prototype: ERR bmp::CopyArea(OBJECTPTR Object, objBitmap * DestBitmap, BAF Flags, INT X, INT Y, INT Width, INT Height, INT XDest, INT YDest)
method.tiriPrototype: err = CopyArea(DestBitmap:obj, Flags:num, X:num, Y:num, Width:num, Height:num, XDest:num, YDest:num)

input.param: The target bitmap.
Optional flags.
The horizontal position of the area to be copied.
The vertical position of the area to be copied.
The width of the area.
The height of the area.
The horizontal position to copy the area to.
The vertical position to copy the area to.

description.p: CopyArea() copies a rectangular region from this bitmap to DestBitmap. The source rectangle starts at X, Y and has the supplied Width and Height; the destination position is XDest, YDest.
The operation is implemented by CopyArea and supports the same BAF options.

result.error: The target bitmap is not a close enough match to the source bitmap in order to perform the operation.

input.param: Retains the compression data if true.

description.p: Decompress() restores Data for a bitmap previously compressed with Compress. If no compressed data is available, the method returns ERR::Okay without changing the bitmap.
Compressed data is released after a successful restore unless RetainData is true. Retaining it allows the most recent compressed image to be restored again later.

result.error: Insufficient memory in recreating the bitmap data buffer.
A Compression object could not be created.

method.name: Demultiply
method.comment: Reverses the conversion process performed by Premultiply().

description.p: Demultiply() restores straight RGB channel values after Premultiply has converted them to premultiplied alpha. The method returns ERR::NothingDone if BMF::PREMUL is not set in Flags.
This method operates only on 32-bit bitmaps that have an alpha channel, and it processes only the current clipping region.

result.error: The Bitmap is not in the expected state (32-bit with an alpha channel).

method.name: DrawRectangle
method.comment: Draws rectangles, both filled and unfilled.
method.prototype: ERR bmp::DrawRectangle(OBJECTPTR Object, INT X, INT Y, INT Width, INT Height, UINT Colour, BAF Flags)
method.tiriPrototype: err = DrawRectangle(X:num, Y:num, Width:num, Height:num, Colour:num, Flags:num)

input.param: The left-most coordinate of the rectangle.
The top-most coordinate of the rectangle.
The width of the rectangle.
The height of the rectangle.
The colour index to use for the rectangle.
Supports FILL and BLEND.

description.p: This method draws both filled and unfilled rectangles. The rectangle is drawn to the target bitmap at position (X, Y) with dimensions determined by the specified Width and Height. If the Flags parameter sets the FILL flag then the rectangle will be filled, otherwise the rectangle's outline will be drawn. The colour of the rectangle is determined by the pixel value in the Colour parameter.
The draw operation is clipped to the bitmap's current clipping region.

method.name: GetColour
method.comment: Converts Red, Green, Blue components into a single colour value.
method.prototype: ERR bmp::GetColour(OBJECTPTR Object, INT Red, INT Green, INT Blue, INT Alpha, UINT * Colour)
method.tiriPrototype: err, Colour:num = GetColour(Red:num, Green:num, Blue:num, Alpha:num)

input.param: Red component from 0 - 255.
Green component from 0 - 255.
Blue component value from 0 - 255.
Alpha component value from 0 - 255.
The resulting colour value will be returned here.

description.p: The GetColour() method is used to convert Red, Green, Blue and Alpha colour components into a single colour index that can be used for directly writing colours to the bitmap. The result is returned in the Colour parameter.

method.name: Premultiply
method.comment: Premultiplies RGB channel values by the alpha channel.

description.p: Premultiply() converts RGB values in the current clipping region to premultiplied-alpha form. The formula applied to each colour channel is (Colour * Alpha + 0xff)>>8. The alpha channel is not changed.
This method operates only on 32-bit bitmaps that have an alpha channel. If the bitmap is already marked as premultiplied, the method returns ERR::NothingDone.
The process can be reversed with a call to Demultiply.

result.error: The Bitmap is not in the expected state (32-bit with an alpha channel)

method.name: SetClipRegion
method.comment: Sets a clipping region for a bitmap object.
method.prototype: ERR bmp::SetClipRegion(OBJECTPTR Object, INT Number, INT Left, INT Top, INT Right, INT Bottom, INT Terminate)
method.tiriPrototype: err = SetClipRegion(Number:num, Left:num, Top:num, Right:num, Bottom:num, Terminate:num)

input.param: The number of the clip region to set.
The horizontal start of the clip region.
The vertical start of the clip region.
The exclusive right edge of the clip region.
The exclusive bottom edge of the clip region.
Set to true if this is the last clip region in the list, otherwise false.

description.p: SetClipRegion() updates one entry in the bitmap's clipping region list. Drawing operations are restricted to the combined region.
This method is implemented by SetClipRegion.

field.name: AmtColours
field.comment: The maximum number of colours represented by the bitmap format.

description.p: For indexed bitmaps, this is the size of the usable palette. For direct-colour bitmaps, it reflects the colour range implied by BitsPerPixel and the selected ColourFormat.

field.name: BitsPerPixel
field.comment: The number of bits used to represent each pixel.

description.p: This includes all bits used by the pixel format, including alpha bits where present.

field.name: Bkgd
field.comment: Background colour in RGB format.

description.p: The background colour is used by operations that need a default fill colour, such as Clear, Draw and some resize paths. The default background colour is black.
The BkgdIndex will be updated as a result of setting this field.

field.name: BkgdIndex
field.comment: Background colour as a packed pixel value or palette index.

description.p: Use Bkgd for most updates. Set BkgdIndex directly only when the caller has already calculated the target bitmap's native pixel value or palette index.

field.name: BlendMode
field.comment: Defines the blending algorithm to use when rendering transparent pixels.

description.p: The default value is BLM::AUTO, which selects the preferred blending path for the current bitmap and graphics backend.

field.name: ByteWidth
field.comment: The width of the bitmap, in bytes.

description.p: ByteWidth is calculated from Width, Type and BytesPerPixel. It describes the meaningful pixel bytes in a row and does not include alignment padding.
The formulas used to calculate the value of this field are:
description.pre: Planar = Width/8 Chunky/8 = Width Chunky/15 = Width * 2 Chunky/16 = Width * 2 Chunky/24 = Width * 3 Chunky/32 = Width * 4
description.p: To learn the total byte-width per line including any additional padded bytes, refer to the LineWidth field.

field.name: BytesPerPixel
field.comment: The number of bytes per pixel.

description.p: This field reflects the byte count used by one chunky pixel. Values normally range from 1 to 4. For planar bitmaps, BitsPerPixel is the more useful format indicator.

field.name: Clip
field.comment: Defines the bitmap's clipping region.

description.p: Clip is a shorthand reference for ClipLeft, ClipTop, ClipRight and ClipBottom, returning all four values as a single ClipRectangle structure.

field.name: ClipBottom
field.comment: The exclusive bottom edge of the bitmap clipping region.

description.p: The default clipping region matches the bitmap dimensions. Drawing operations are limited to the active clipping region.

field.name: ClipLeft
field.comment: The left-most edge of a bitmap's clipping region.

description.p: The default clipping region matches the bitmap dimensions. Drawing operations are limited to the active clipping region.

field.name: ClipRight
field.comment: The exclusive right edge of the bitmap clipping region.

description.p: The default clipping region matches the bitmap dimensions. Drawing operations are limited to the active clipping region.

field.name: ClipTop
field.comment: The top-most edge of a bitmap's clipping region.

description.p: The default clipping region matches the bitmap dimensions. Drawing operations are limited to the active clipping region.

field.name: ColourFormat
field.comment: Describes the colour format used to construct each bitmap pixel.

description.p: ColourFormat points to the structure that describes how packed pixel values map to red, green, blue and alpha channels. It is relevant for direct-colour bitmaps, normally those with two or more bytes per pixel.
The following C++ helper methods can be called on a bitmap to build packed colour values from channel components:
description.pre: packPixel(Red, Green, Blue) packPixel(Red, Green, Blue, Alpha) packAlpha(Alpha) packPixelRGB(RGB8 &RGB) packPixelRGBA(RGB8 &RGB)
description.p: The following C macros are optimised forms for 24 and 32-bit bitmaps:
description.pre: PackPixelWB(Red, Green, Blue) PackPixelWBA(Red, Green, Blue, Alpha)
description.p: The following C++ helper methods unpack individual colour components from a packed colour value:

field.name: ColourSpace
field.comment: Defines the colour space for RGB values.

field.name: Data
field.comment: Pointer to a bitmap's data area.

description.p: Data points to the first byte of the bitmap's pixel buffer when CPU-visible memory is available. Caller-supplied memory can be used for data-backed bitmaps, but most callers should let Init allocate the correctly sized buffer.
For video or texture-backed bitmaps, Data may be unavailable until Lock succeeds.

field.name: DataFlags
field.comment: Defines the memory flags to use in allocating a bitmap's data area.

description.p: DataFlags controls the kind of backing storage requested during initialisation. Common values are MEM::DATA, MEM::VIDEO and MEM::TEXTURE.

Video or texture-backed bitmaps can be faster for some drawing paths, but direct CPU access is platform dependent. Use Lock before reading or writing Data directly when the bitmap is not a regular data bitmap.

field.name: DrawUCPixel
field.comment: Points to a C function that draws pixels to the bitmap using colour indexes.

description.p: DrawUCPixel points to the active low-level pixel writer for packed colour or palette-index values. It is intended for C callers that need direct pixel access. No clipping or bounds checks are performed.
The prototype of the DrawUCPixel function is Function(*Bitmap, LONG X, LONG Y, uint32_t Colour).
The new pixel value is supplied in the Colour parameter.

field.name: DrawUCRIndex
field.comment: Points to a C function that draws pixels to the bitmap in RGB format.

description.p: DrawUCRIndex points to the active low-level RGB pixel writer for a caller-supplied address inside Data. It is intended for C callers that need direct pixel access. No clipping, bounds or address validation is performed.
The prototype of the DrawUCRIndex function is Function(*Bitmap, uint8_t *Data, RGB8 *RGB).
The Data parameter must point to a location within the Bitmap's graphical address space. The new pixel value must be defined in the RGB parameter.
There is no colour-index equivalent because callers can write indexed pixel bytes directly through Data.

field.name: DrawUCRPixel
field.comment: Points to a C function that draws pixels to the bitmap in RGB format.

description.p: DrawUCRPixel points to the active low-level RGB pixel writer for X, Y coordinates. It is intended for C callers that need direct pixel access. No clipping or bounds checks are performed.
The prototype of the DrawUCRPixel function is Function(*Bitmap, LONG X, LONG Y, RGB8 *RGB).
The new pixel value must be defined in the RGB parameter.

field.name: Flags
field.comment: Optional flags.

field.name: Height
field.comment: The height of the bitmap, in pixels.

field.name: LineWidth
field.comment: The length of each bitmap line in bytes, including alignment.

description.p: LineWidth includes any row padding required by the active bitmap type or platform backend. Use ByteWidth for the number of meaningful pixel bytes in a row.

field.name: Opacity
field.comment: Determines the translucency setting to use in drawing operations.

description.p: Opacity is an 8-bit alpha multiplier used by drawing operations that support translucent bitmap copies. A value of 255 is fully opaque and disables additional translucency. Lower values make copied pixels more transparent.
This value is separate from any per-pixel alpha channel stored in the bitmap.

field.name: Palette
field.comment: Points to a bitmap's colour palette.

description.p: Palette points to the bitmap's colour table. Indexed bitmaps use this table to map pixel values to RGB colours, and some conversion paths use it even when the bitmap itself is direct-colour.
The structure starts with the palette header and colour count, followed by colour entries in index order. There is no terminating entry.
The following example is for a 32 colour palette:
Palettes are created for all bitmap types, including RGB bitmaps above 8-bit colour, because several drawing functions use a palette table when converting between bitmap formats.
Parent objects such as Display may need to be updated separately before palette changes are reflected by the visible display.

field.name: PlaneMod
field.comment: The differential between each bitmap plane.

description.p: PlaneMod specifies the byte distance between each bitplane in planar bitmaps. For chunky bitmaps, it reflects the total size of the bitmap buffer.

field.name: Position
field.comment: The current read/write data position.

description.p: Position is the byte offset used by Read and Write. Use Seek to change it.

field.name: ReadUCPixel
field.comment: Points to a C function that reads pixels from the bitmap in colour index format.

description.p: ReadUCPixel points to the active low-level pixel reader for packed colour or palette-index values. It is intended for C callers that need direct pixel access. No clipping or bounds checks are performed.
The prototype of the ReadUCPixel function is Function(*Bitmap, LONG X, LONG Y, LONG *Index).
The pixel value will be returned in the Index parameter.

field.name: ReadUCRIndex
field.comment: Points to a C function that reads pixels from the bitmap in RGB format.

description.p: ReadUCRIndex points to the active low-level RGB pixel reader for a caller-supplied address inside Data. It is intended for C callers that need direct pixel access. No clipping, bounds or address validation is performed.
The prototype of the ReadUCRIndex function is Function(*Bitmap, uint8_t *Data, RGB8 *RGB).
The Data parameter must point to a location within the Bitmap's graphical address space. The pixel value will be returned in the RGB parameter.
There is no colour-index equivalent because callers can read indexed pixel bytes directly through Data.

field.name: ReadUCRPixel
field.comment: Points to a C function that reads pixels from the bitmap in RGB format.

description.p: ReadUCRPixel points to the active low-level RGB pixel reader for X, Y coordinates. It is intended for C callers that need direct pixel access. No clipping or bounds checks are performed.
The prototype of the ReadUCRPixel function is Function(*Bitmap, LONG X, LONG Y, RGB8 *RGB).
The pixel value is returned in the RGB parameter. Because this function expands the pixel value to RGB components, ReadUCPixel or ReadUCRIndex may be faster when RGB decomposition is not required.

field.name: Size
field.comment: The total size of the bitmap, in bytes.

field.name: TransColour
field.comment: The transparent colour of the bitmap, in RGB format.

description.p: Pixels matching this colour are skipped by drawing operations that honour colour-key transparency.
Do not use colour-key transparency on bitmaps that use alpha transparency.

field.name: TransIndex
field.comment: The transparent colour of the bitmap, represented as an index.

description.p: TransIndex stores the transparent colour as a packed pixel value or palette index. Pixels matching this value are skipped by drawing operations that honour colour-key transparency.

Use TransColour for most updates. Set TransIndex directly only when the caller has already calculated the target bitmap's native pixel value or palette index. Do not use colour-key transparency on bitmaps that use alpha transparency.

field.name: Type
field.comment: Defines the data type of the bitmap.

description.p: Type defines the bitmap layout, either BMP::PLANAR for planar bitmaps or BMP::CHUNKY for interleaved pixel data. Chunky is the default.

field.name: Width
field.comment: The width of the bitmap, in pixels.

description.p: Width must be set before Query or Init can derive the bitmap layout.

constants.const: Enable alpha blending to the destination if the source supports an alpha channel.
Special CopyArea option that avoids blending when the destination pixel is empty.
Perform dithering if the colour formats differ between the source and destination.
For primitive operations such as DrawRectangle, this will fill the shape with a solid colour or texture.
Use linear interpolation to improve the quality of alpha blending.

constants.comment: Defines the blending algorithm to use when transparent pixels are rendered to the bitmap.
constants.const: Use the most suitable of the available algorithms.
Use gamma correct blending. This algorithm is slow but produces a high quality result.
Use linear blending. Applicable if the bitmap is in linear colour space.
Never blend transparent pixels, just copy as-is.
Use sRGB linear blending. This algorithm is extremely efficient but produces poor quality results.
2D video acceleration is available.
3D video acceleration is available.
For 32-bit images, indicates that an alpha channel is present.
Forces a blank/black palette on initialisation.
Clear graphics on initialisation and when resizing.
The bitmap data is compressed.
Prevent changing of bitmap depth after initialisation (e.g. via Resize()).
Indicates reverse alpha blending, higher values are transparent.
Declare the Bitmap as a 1 or 8 bit mask. Must be set in conjunction with the Bitmap.BitsPerPixel field on initialisation.
Ignore resize requests that would shrink the size of the bitmap.
Do not allocate memory in the Data field on initialisation.
The RGB values are premultiplied (32-bit only).
Automatically set after a Query() on the bitmap.
Indicates that the bitmap utilises a transparent colour. This is automatically set if the Bitmap.TransIndex or Bitmap.TransColour is defined, and support exists in functions such as CopyArea.
This user flag can be used to tag bitmaps with special meaning. Not used internally.
Chunky pixel mode (default).
Planar pixel mode separates pixel bits across multiple planes. Commonly used for single bit bitmap masks.
Cartesian L*a*b* colour space defined by CIE 15.
Polar L*CHab colour space defined by CIE 15.
Linear RGB is used to improve colour balance in blending operations.
The default colour-space is sRGB.

struct.name: ColourFormat
struct.field: Right shift value for red (15/16 bit formats only)
Unshifted mask value for red (ranges from 0x00 to 0xff)
Number of bits per pixel for this format.
